seoTitle: Integrate Figma Designs Into Squarespace: Full Guide
metaDescription: Turn your Figma prototypes into a live Squarespace website by learning how to export assets, adjust layouts, and match your design vision.
intro: Bridging the gap between a Figma design and a working Squarespace site can feel tricky if you're used to designing in one tool and building in another. This tutorial covers exporting your Figma assets, importing them into Squarespace, and adjusting layouts so the finished site matches your original design intent.

steps.heading: Export your Figma assets
steps.body: Prepare your images, icons, and graphics for export from Figma in formats that will work well on the web, keeping file sizes reasonable for fast loading.

steps.heading: Import assets into Squarespace
steps.body: Upload your exported Figma assets into your Squarespace site and place them into the relevant pages, whether that's a landing page, portfolio, or e-commerce layout.

steps.heading: Customize layout and styling
steps.body: Rebuild your Figma layout using Squarespace's section and block tools, matching spacing, alignment, and typography as closely as possible to the original design.

steps.heading: Optimize images and graphics
steps.body: Compress and resize images so the page loads quickly without sacrificing the visual quality of your Figma-designed graphics.

steps.heading: Review responsive behavior
steps.body: Check how the imported design displays across desktop and mobile, adjusting layout blocks as needed for a consistent experience on any screen.

faq.question: Can I import a Figma file directly into Squarespace?
faq.answer: Not as a single file — you export assets and layouts from Figma, then rebuild the structure using Squarespace's page and section tools.

faq.question: Will my Figma fonts carry over exactly?
faq.answer: Not automatically; you'll need to match or substitute fonts available in Squarespace to stay close to your original Figma typography.

faq.question: Is this suitable for a first-time Squarespace user?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial is designed for beginners as well as designers and business owners who want to translate a design into a live site.

faq.question: Can I use this approach for an e-commerce site?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial mentions using this workflow for custom landing pages, portfolios, and e-commerce sites built from Figma prototypes.

faq.question: Are there tools that automate this process?
faq.answer: The tutorial touches on automation options and AI tools that can help streamline moving a design from Figma into a built website.
